contentBlocks.title: Why convert SVG to JPG?
contentBlocks.html:
SVG is a vector format ideal for logos and icons, but many platforms only accept raster images. JPG produces smaller files than PNG, making it a good choice when transparency is not needed.

Converting SVG to JPG is useful for email attachments, website images, social media posts, and any context where a compact, universally compatible image is required.

Note that JPG does not support transparency — transparent SVG areas will be filled with a white background. If you need transparency, use our SVG to PNG converter instead.

items.title: 2. Set quality

items.title: 3. Download JPG
items.description: Download each file or grab them all at once. Size comparison shown for every file.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about SVG to JPG conversion

items.question: Does converting SVG to JPG lose quality?
items.answer: JPG is lossy, so some detail is discarded. At 85% quality the result looks excellent. Transparent areas become white.

items.question: Can I adjust the output quality?
items.answer: Yes. Use the slider to set quality from 60% to 95%.

items.question: Is transparency preserved?
items.answer: No. JPG does not support transparency. Transparent areas are filled with white. Use SVG to PNG if you need transparency.

items.question: Can I convert multiple files at once?
Are files uploaded to a server?
items.answer: No. Everything runs locally in your browser.

items.question: What is the maximum file size?
items.answer: Individual files up to 100 MB are supported.
